pattern: After sorting points by day, a new line segment is needed only where the slope changes, so counting slope changes (via cross products to avoid division) gives the answer.
hints: Sort the points by day first; consecutive points form the chart left to right.
Three consecutive points lie on one line when the segment slopes match. Compare slopes without floating point by cross-multiplying the deltas.
Start the count at one line for the first two points (if there are at least two), then add a line each time the slope of (i-1,i) differs from (i-2,i-1).

edge_inputs.description: three direction changes produce three lines
unsorted collinear points form a single line
two points always make exactly one line
flat horizontal run is one line
slope must be compared without division to dodge rounding
steep then shallow change forces a new line

stress.description: many points with random days and prices, often collinear by chance
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    days = rng.sample(range(1, 10**9 + 1), n)
    pts = [[d, rng.randint(1, 10**9)] for d in days]
    return [pts]
stress.note: distinct days as required; cross-product slope comparison must hold at large magnitudes
